title: G. E. M. Anscombe - On Wittgenstein
text:
well i i can tell you something
he had an extraordinary understanding of
why people thought
the things that they did think
in philosophical argument
so that when he undermined it
his undermining showed that he was
getting at the
the nerve the root
it was not a superficial
refutation
he also was uh
struck when as a great deal more alive
than almost anybody else
uh he also had an amazingly good
judgment
of what what it was sensible to tell
somebody to read
what was right for them
thank you thank you very much thank you
